Create and manage spending budgets per category or overall. All require authentication.

Create a new budget. `categoryId` is optional — omit it (or set null) for an overall budget. `period`: DAILY | WEEKLY | MONTHLY | YEARLY. `alertThreshold`: fraction (0.0–1.0) at which a BUDGET_ALERT notification fires.
Returns all active, non-deleted budgets for the authenticated user with real-time spent/remaining amounts.
Returns real-time budget utilisation for a single budget: spent, remaining, percentUsed, isOverBudget, isNearLimit.

Returns month-over-month spending totals for the last N calendar months. Each month includes totalAmount, expenseCount, and a category breakdown with percentages. Months with zero spending are included with 0 values. Requires SUPER_ADMIN role.

description.content:
Read-only audit trail of all entity changes.
GET /       → ADMIN
GET /me     → any authenticated user
GET /user   → SUPER_ADMIN
GET /entity → ADMIN
GET /action → ADMIN
Returns all audit logs paginated. Requires ADMIN role.
Returns audit logs for the authenticated user only. Available to any authenticated user.
Returns audit logs for any user by ID. Requires SUPER_ADMIN role.
Returns full change history for a specific entity. `entity_type`: Expense | Budget | RecurringExpense | Category. Requires ADMIN role.

Filter audit logs by action type. `audit_action` values: EXPENSE_CREATED | EXPENSE_UPDATED | EXPENSE_DELETED | EXPENSE_AUTO_CREATED | BUDGET_CREATED | BUDGET_UPDATED | BUDGET_DELETED | RECURRING_EXPENSE_CREATED | RECURRING_EXPENSE_UPDATED | RECURRING_EXPENSE_DELETED | RECURRING_EXPENSE_PROCESSED | CATEGORY_CREATED | CATEGORY_UPDATED | CATEGORY_DELETED | USER_REGISTERED | USER_LOGIN | ROLE_ASSIGNED | NOTIFICATION_READ | NOTIFICATION_DELETED. Requires ADMIN role.
In-app notifications triggered by budget alerts, budget exceeded events, and recurring expense processing. All require authentication.
Returns all notifications for the authenticated user, newest first (paginated).

response.name: 200 OK

description.content: Returns all unread notifications (not paginated). Use for badge display.
Returns the integer count of unread notifications. Lightweight endpoint for badge counters.
Mark a single notification as read. Returns 404 if it doesn't belong to the authenticated user. Audit logged as NOTIFICATION_READ.
Marks all unread notifications as read in one call. Returns count of updated records.
Permanently delete a notification. Returns 404 if it doesn't belong to the authenticated user. Audit logged as NOTIFICATION_DELETED.
Single aggregated financial snapshot for the authenticated user.
Returns: this-month spending summary, budget overview with utilisation, last 5 expenses, upcoming recurring expenses (next 7 days), category breakdown with percentages, and unread notification count.

response.name: 200 OK

description.content: Auto-filled by the Login request test script
Used by SUPER_ADMIN endpoints that target another user
Expense | Budget | RecurringExpense | Category
Any AuditAction enum value

Single collection covering every endpoint in the Expense Tracker Spring Boot application.

Features:
- Auth (register, login, roles, assign-roles)
- Expenses (CRUD + search + filters)
- Categories (admin global + user-specific)
- Budgets (CRUD + status)
- Recurring Expenses (CRUD)
- Reports (summary, category-wise, date-wise, custom, monthly trends, budget performance)
- Audit Logs (all, mine, by-user, by-entity, by-action)
- Notifications (list, unread, mark-read, delete)
- Dashboard

Setup:
1. Import this collection.
2. Set collection variable `base_url` to your server (default: http://localhost:8081).
3. Run 'Login' under Auth — the test script auto-saves the JWT to `token`.
4. All other requests inherit Bearer auth from the collection level.
Create a new user account. Returns JWT token + user info.
Create a new user account. Returns JWT token + user info.
Authenticate and receive a JWT. The test script auto-saves the token to the `token` collection variable.
